Chapter 12 — Using Excel in Accounting a — All time-value-of-money calculations rest on five variables. Excel has isolated them and made each into a function. Know

beats.text: Money changes value over time. This is not a theory. It is written into every interest rate, every loan contract, every investment decision. A dollar in your hand today is worth more than a dollar next year, because today you can put it to work , invest it, lend it, spend it and avoid borrowing later. Wait a year, and that possibility is gone. All time-value-of-money calculations rest on five variables. Excel has isolated them and made each into a function.

segments.label: 01 / 11 · HOOK

segments.section: NET PRESENT VALUE: WHY A DOLLA
title: NET PRESENT VALUE:
WHY A DOLLAR IN YEAR FIVE IS NOT A DOLLAR TODAY
segments.body_html:

When The Two Methods Agree — When The Two Methods Agree — first option applied to the chapter example.

beats.text: A company is considering two pieces of equipment. Both require money today and generate cash over four years. Which is the better investment? You cannot compare them by simply adding up the cash flows and comparing totals. A dollar received in year four is worth less than a dollar received in year one, because the year-one dollar has three more years to compound. To compare cash flows at different times, you have to discount them back to a common moment , today.

beats.text: Equipment loses value as you use it. The tax code recognizes this and allows you to deduct that loss as a business expense, reducing taxable income each year over the asset's life. Excel automates the arithmetic. Three variables govern every depreciation calculation:

beats.text: A financial statement in Excel is a system of formulas, not a list of numbers. This is the design principle that separates a model you can trust from a model that breaks silently.

segments.section: THE HARD LINE BETWEEN EXCEL AN
title: THE HARD LINE BETWEEN EXCEL AND
ACCOUNTING SOFTWARE
segments.body_html:
Excel is for analysis and modeling. It is not a ledger.

The Hard Line Between Excel and Accounti — This distinction is not a preference. It is structural. Accounting software — QuickBooks, SAP, Oracle — enforces the rul

beats.text: Excel is for analysis and modeling. It is not a ledger. This distinction is not a preference. It is structural. Accounting software , QuickBooks, SAP, Oracle , enforces the rules of double-entry bookkeeping: every transaction is recorded once and only once, debits and credits always balance, the chart of accounts stays consistent across periods. When you enter a transaction, the software enforces these constraints.

title: WHAT VERIFICATION ACTUALLY
LOOKS LIKE.
segments.body_html:
Every concept in this chapter has a verification habit attached to it. This is not optional overhead. It is the work.

What Verification Actually Looks Like — For time-value-of-money functions: check the units. The interest rate and the number of periods must be in the same time

beats.text: Every concept in this chapter has a verification habit attached to it. This is not optional overhead. It is the work. For time-value-of-money functions: check the units. The interest rate and the number of periods must be in the same time unit. An annual rate with monthly periods gives a payment that is far too low. Run the plausibility check: is the output higher than the principal divided by the number of periods? If not, something is wrong.

beats.text: A company wants to finance a $180,000 piece of equipment at 6 percent annual interest over 4 years. Write the PMT formula to calculate the annual payment. Then rewrite it to calculate the monthly payment. What two things change between the annual and monthly versions, and why must they both change together? Tests: the unit-alignment rule , RATE and NPER must be in the same time unit, and changing one without the other produces a nonsense answer. Difficulty: low.

segments.label: 07 / 11 · EXERCISES

title: AI WAYBACK
MACHINE.
segments.body_html:
Ask it to explain how the debit/credit logic of 1494 maps onto a modern Excel income statement and balance sheetwhat's the same, what's different, what's better, what's worse
Ask"Pacioli was a Franciscan friar, a mathematics teacher, and a friend of Leonardo da Vinci. How did each of those roles s
Add the framing"Answer as if you're a Venetian merchant in 1500 explaining why your apprentice should learn this new bookkeeping method

beats.text: The ideas in this chapter didn't appear from nowhere. Luca Pacioli was working on Summa de Arithmetica (1494) , the first published description of double-entry bookkeeping, the accounting method every modern Excel financial model still uses decades before most people had heard of accounting and financial reporting in spreadsheets. Here's a prompt to find out more , and then make it better.

Prompts — Create a single-file D3 v7 HTML process using 4-6 labeled nodes or panels, directional connectors where sequence matters

beats.text: Create a single-file D3 v7 HTML process using 4-6 labeled nodes or panels, directional connectors where sequence matters, direct labels on every mark, one primary red emphasis mark, neutral supporting marks, and no unlabeled boxes. The figure should represent: a five-year income statement flow diagram showing how the growth rate assumption feeds into revenue in each year, and how revenue feeds into COGS, gross profit, operating profit, and net profit , with arrows showing the formula dependencies, making visible which cells are inputs and which are calculations.
